rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Year/number to the far right partly cropped; text 'Arrearagia pro 2do die Novembris' is legible though the trailing numerals are ambiguous.
Clear header phrase written under the title: 'Money Rents fixed  Half year'. Very legible.
The pounds '30' in the leftmost of the three amount columns is clearly written and aligns with this row. Shillings/pence columns are blank/zero in the ruled columns.
Description reads 'Munkennott Do' (the second word abbreviated 'Do'). Pound value '20' is clear; shillings/pence not present.
Entry 'Kidlington Do' (Do = ditto). Pound figure '20' clearly aligned in pounds column.
Name reads 'Lucas Eaton'. Pound value '4' is visible in the pounds column; shillings/pence not recorded.
The name 'Bampton' is legible but the amount area for this row is mostly blank or overwritten; no clear numerals found for pounds/shillings/pence.
Reading of 'Montacute' is reasonably clear. A small '2' aligns with the pounds column for this row; shillings/pence absent. Ink slightly faded here.
Description 'Jesus College' is readable. The amount to the right is faint/obscured and not unambiguously extractable from the image.

Text appears 'Hertford College'. To the far right there is a grouped amount that appears as '3 10' (3 pounds 10 shillings). The pence column is blank; handwriting is somewhat cramped so shilling reading has moderate uncertainty.
Description reads like 'Richd St Martins' (abbreviation 'Richd' likely Richard). A '4' is visible in the pounds column; shillings/pence not present. Moderate legibility of name.
The name is uncertain (read as 'Tetterwin' or similar). No clear amount visible aligned to this line; ink is cramped and slightly overlapped by adjacent annotation.
Description is very hard to decipher (reads 'Back gate ?spin' or 'Backgate ?spin'). The amount area for this row is unclear/blank. Low confidence in transcription of description.
Text reads like 'Bad Lane Compre' (possibly 'Bad Lane Compre/Comprese'). Amount fields faint or omitted; description legible but unusual spelling.
A 'Do' (ditto) entry follows; text to its right (possibly a surname) is faint. No amount clearly readable for this line.
The description includes 'now Pickford' (legible). The far-right amount appears as '26 6 8' (26 pounds, 6 shillings, 8 pence) — digits are fairly clear though the handwriting is slightly cursive.
Name reads 'Medenham' (likely 'Medham/Medenham'). The amount '1 13 4' is reasonably clear in the three amount columns.
Description 'Lycrofts Close' (handwritten). Amount to the right reads '13 6 8' fairly clearly in the three columns.
This line appears to be a header or subsection label 'Little & War' (possibly abbreviation). No direct amount on the same line.
Under the 'Little' subsection, 'Kimber' is listed with amount '4 5 0' clearly in pounds/shillings columns; pence blank.
Name reads 'Gardiner, Hester'. Rightmost columns read '1 6 8' — digits legible though the comma and second name are slightly slanted.

Line begins with 'Do' (ditto). The surname following looks like 'Evans' and there is a numeral in the shillings column '6' but the pounds column is faint/absent for this row; I left pounds blank due to ambiguity.
Name 'Beck' is clear. Far-right columns show '2 12 2' — shillings/pence are readable though the '12' shillings is valid (12<20). Slight smudge on the pence makes it moderate confidence.
Surname reads 'Barnesle' (handwriting could be 'Barswell' or 'Barnesle'). Amount '2 6 0' visible in the columns; pence blank.
Name 'Leonard' clear. Amount '2 6 0' aligns with this row in the amount columns.
Name uncertain (reads 'Cunzock' or 'Cunzocke'); a '1' is visible in the pounds column for this line. Shillings/pence blank.
Clear subsection header 'Land Tax Redeemed' written in larger script.
Entry 'Kidlington Rectory' with amount clearly written as '22 5 6' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
'Do Vicarage' (ditto). Amount reads '2 12 6' but the initial '2' (pounds) is smaller; moderate confidence due to cramped digits.
Description 'Wittenham Rectory' readable. Amount appears as '12 7 6' in the three columns though the pence is slightly smudged.
Another 'Do' line; to the right there is a heavier numeral '29' but the exact alignment between pounds/shillings is uncertain (possibly '29 0 0' or part of another subtotal). Low confidence.
Section header 'Mixed Rents Variable' (handwritten 'Mixed Rents (Variable)') — clear and centered.
Name 'Long Wittenham Rectory' is legible. Amount appears as '122 7 9' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; digits are cramped but readable.
Line reads 'John Newington Do' (surname and 'Do' abbreviation). Right-hand columns read '103 17 10' but shillings '17' and pence '10' are written tightly; moderate confidence.
Reads 'Norton Do' with amount '10 10 1' — digits visible though slightly compressed; pence '1' could be 1 or 2 but chosen as 1 based on alignment.
Name appears 'Gaynton Do & Tenement' (handwriting ambiguous). Amount looks like '41 17 3' though the pounds '41' could be '4l' with a flourish; moderate uncertainty.
Entry 'Chase Hill' with amount '13 14 4' visible. Handwriting legible but slightly faded on the pence column.
Name 'Kidlington Mills' clear. Amount '20 2 4' appears in the right-hand columns; digits clear.
Surname appears as 'Gardiner - Clinchard' (second name possibly 'Clinkard'/'Clinchard'). Amount '10 2 2' visible but some letters in name uncertain.
A 'Do' entry with a following surname that reads 'Truin' (or 'Truin/Truin'). Amount appears as '10 1 2' though the pence column is small and a little unclear.
Line seems to read 'Toninhille - Napier' and the amount '8 8 2' is visible in the amount columns. Some uncertainty in the place name spelling.
'Do - Beaton' line shows amount '2 17 9' but the leading '2' is small and could be part of a running total; chosen based on column alignment.
Name 'Clifton Ferry' is fairly clear. Amount reads '9 5 9' in the columns; digits legible though slightly cramped.
Entry (looks like 'Emsington' or 'Emsington Tenement'). Amount '9 17 6' readable but handwriting is tight causing moderate uncertainty.
Description is difficult: reads 'Southlet & Berington Tenement' (uncertain). Amount looks like '7 3 6' but due to uncertain description the confidence is lower.

Line reads 'Little & Ww - Taylor'. The amount appears as '24 10 8½' — pence shows a small loop/'ob' indicating a halfpenny; interpreted as 8 pence and 0.5 fraction. Slight uncertainty on fraction reading.
Entry 'Judge' clear. Amount reads '11 12 3' in the columns; pence legible, neat writing.

Surname could be 'Hidens' or 'Hildens'. Amount looks like '11 3 7¾' or '11 3 7¼' — the small symbol after the pence resembles a farthing sign ('q'/'qd'), interpreted here as 0.25 (one farthing). The fraction interpretation is moderately uncertain.
Reads 'Gardiner (Burdons)' with amount '18 14 9' visible; digits are clear though parentheses are scribbled.
Final listed name 'Hartley' with amount '3 3 10' in the amount columns; clear handwriting at the bottom-right.

rows.description: Mem: These are in reality the proper Arrearagia for the conclusion of the Computations of 1000L.
rows.notes: Bottom explanatory note. The sentence is legible though several words (e.g. final numerals) are slightly abbreviated; treated as a footer/header with no amounts.
